issues_found: Topic metadata claims 'market structure' but source content is about household debt/consumer credit trends, not market structure (trading mechanisms, market plumbing, operations)
Source is from 2012 (approximately 14 years old), far exceeding the 90-day threshold
Event type 'data_release_other' is technically valid but borderline given archive focus on market structure and macro catalysts
human_review_reason: Source content is factually accurate and well-parsed, but has significant relevance concerns: (1) Topic classification mismatch between metadata ('market structure') and actual content (household debt/consumer credit), (2) Age of 14 years makes it a borderline candidate for archival value assessment. Human decision needed on whether consumer credit/household debt data qualifies under the archive's scope and whether historical data from 2012 has sufficient archival value despite age.

corrected_fields.why_it_matters: This quarterly data release documents household deleveraging trends following the 2008 financial crisis. Student loan debt was the sole category growing while other consumer debt categories declined, indicating structural shifts in household balance sheets. Note: The archive scope question here is whether household debt/consumer credit data falls under 'market structure' as defined (trading mechanisms, market operations, plumbing) or represents a separate category.
corrected_fields.macro_context: Historical household debt data from Q1 2012 showing consumer deleveraging trends during the post-2008 recovery period. Student loan debt was uniquely growing while other debt categories (mortgages, credit cards) were contracting since the Q3 2008 peak. The 90+ day delinquency rate for student loans (8.69%) was higher than mortgages, auto loans, and HELOCs.
